response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering skills with 16+ years of experience and solid C/Linux knowledge, they lack critical required qualifications for this role. Most critically, they have no native-level Japanese language proficiency (explicitly required), no embedded systems or firmware development experience, and no demonstrated expertise in security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption) or networking domains (Layer 2/3, SNMP, NETCONF) essential for LAN-switch security development. Their background is predominantly in web/backend systems and UI development, not low-level embedded firmware.
strengths: 16+ years of professional software development experience across multiple domains and languages
Demonstrated proficiency in C/C++ and strong Linux/Unix systems knowledge from roles at National Instruments and infrastructure work
Strong emphasis on software craftsmanship, TDD, and code quality throughout career
concerns: No native-level Japanese language proficiency—a stated required qualification for the role
No demonstrated embedded systems, firmware, or LAN-switch product development experience; background is primarily web/backend and UI-focused
Complete absence of security networking domain expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption, tamper detection, SNMP, NETCONF)
